stemHtml: You are flying a twin-engined aircraft with an AC electrical system fed by two engine driven generators when one generator has a complete failure. Which of the following is correct with regards to the subsequent power distribution?

options.a: In an AC electrical system with a split-bus design, the load from the failed generator is automatically taken over by the remaining generator through the automatic AC bus-transfer function.
options.b: In an AC electrical system with a parallel bus design, the load from the failed generator is directly taken over by the aircraft's battery providing DC electrical power to the bus.
options.c: In an AC electrical system with a split-bus design, there is no need to transfer the busses to a different source, and they will all continue to run normally, but load shedding may be required due to the lower capacity of the single generator.
options.d: In an AC electrical system with a parallel bus design, the load from the failed generator is automatically taken over by the remaining generator through the automatic AC bus-transfer function.
